zh.Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可索爾茲伯里
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Smith於大約1645年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆咸頓時，他的父親Richard Smith24歲，母親Elizabeth Vials34歲。他於1666年8月17日與Sarah Chandler在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可索爾茲伯里結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1712年8月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可索爾茲伯里享年68歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州艾可索爾茲伯里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Smith于大约1645年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆咸頓时，他的父亲Richard Smith24岁，母亲Elizabeth Vials34岁。他于1666年8月17日与Sarah Chandler在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可索爾茲伯里结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1712年8月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可索爾茲伯里享年68岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州艾可索爾茲伯里。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smithe
meanings: English: variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smythe
meanings: English (London): variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: English:
occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First Recorded Slave in Portsmouth
historicEvents.description: In 1645, an African American man taken from Guinea was captured and brought to New Hampshire, he would be counted as the first African American slave in New Hampshire.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Black Heritage Trail in New Hampshire- Our African Heritage

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Smith was born about 1645, in Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, his father, Richard Smith, was 24 and his mother, Elizabeth Vials, was 34. He married Sarah Chandler on 17 August 1666, in Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 14 August 1712, in Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 68, and was buried in Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Smith est né environ 1645, à Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Richard Smith, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Vials, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Chandler le 17 août 1666, à Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 14 août 1712, à Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

Er hat am 17. August 1666 in Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Sarah Chandler geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 14. it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Smith è nato il circa 1645 nel Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Richard Smith, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Vials, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Chandler il 17 agosto 1666, nel Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 14 agosto 1712, nel Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Smithが約1645年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムハンプトンで生まれた時，父Richard Smithは24歳，母Elizabeth Vialsは34歳であった。1666年8月17日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサリスブリーでSarah Chandlerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1712年8月14日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサリスブリーで68歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州エセックスサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay, Bri: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살리스버이
ko.Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Brit: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 함프턴
ko.Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스
ko.Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts, USA: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살리스버이
ko.Hampton, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크 함프턴
ko.Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살리스버이
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Smith(이)가 대략 1645년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 함프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Smith(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Vials(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1666년 8월 17일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살리스버이에서 Sarah Chandler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1712년 8월 14일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살리스버이에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 에세스 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Smith nasceu aproximadamente 1645, em Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, seu pai, Richard Smith, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Vials, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Chandler em 17 de agosto de 1666, em Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de agosto de 1712, em Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Smith родился около 1645 г. в местности хамптон, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Richard Smith было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Vials, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Chandler 17 августа 1666 г. в местности салисбури, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 14 августа 1712 г. в местности салисбури, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности салисбури, эссекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Smith nació aproximadamente 1645, en Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, su papá, Richard Smith, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Vials, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Sarah Chandler el 17 de agosto de 1666, en Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 14 de agosto de 1712, en Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Salisbury, Essex, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
